下周有一个Apple事件，它看起来很可能会看到iPad Pro的更新模型，也许是iPad Mini。
 这很好，因为它让我想到了EEE PC，这是技术历史中最伟大的短期成功案例之一，或者由2000年代后期的科技博客共同共享的集体妄想，从未实际发生过。
   有两种产品到达2007年，从根本上改变了计算：当然，一个是iPhone。第二种，明显更重要的产品是399美元的PC 701.它最初运行了审阅者喜欢的定制Linux操作系统（笔记本电脑Mag的Mark Spoonauer表示，它比任何窗口笔记本的速度较简单十倍），并且通常是预示的一种具有巨大质量吸引力的新型计算机。 Spoonauer：“镑磅，这个星球上最好的价值笔记本。”
 同样，这是一个古怪的小型双磅塑料笔记本电脑，运行了自定义Linux发行版，基本上是各种网站的前面。 （我们还没有发明这句话“云服务”。）
 Windows不允许由Linux出现，因此Microsoft在Microsoft机动中进行了一些，到2008年1月，EEE PC正在运行Windows XP。它也是一个叫做“上网本”的更大类别的一部分，我们都是为了知道上网本的内容。
   稍后，微软创建了一个名为Windows 7 Starter的东西，这是一个用于上网本的Windows的热闹的剪切版本 - 你甚至都甚至允许更改桌面背景！ - 并且上网本爆炸不可阻挡。我的朋友（和缅甸联合创始人）Joanna Stern建造了她职业生涯的早期部分痴迷覆盖了上网本，首先在Laptop Mag，然后在Gizmodo，然后在Engadget和我在一起。
 你还记得诺基亚小册子3g是否要重振诺基亚？你没有，因为它没有。 
还有很多东西要掩盖：乔安娜的一点指出，华硕于2008年推出了20个不同型号的EEE电脑。那只是华硕！戴尔，惠普，联想和其他人在上网本想法疯狂地追逐。你还记得诺基亚小册子3g是否要重振诺基亚？你没有，因为它没有。但是非常漂亮。我问乔安娜及时谈论这一刻，这就是她发给我的：
 “我基本上是Bono，我仍然没有找到我正在寻找的视频。 EEE PC之后PC。 MSI风之后的MSI风。东芝在东芝被称为叫做什么之后的任何东西。我不断寻找一个有一个键盘的上网本，键盘不需要娃娃手，这是一个没有在我的拇指上留下水泡的轨道板，这是一个不需要三天的硬盘，打开微软单词。这是一个不断寻求的价格，可移植性和力量的完美混合。“
 乔安娜然后要求我嵌入了U2的实际YouTube视频“我仍然没有找到我正在寻找的东西”，这正是这种博客帖子所需的“哇”的“哇非常重要的哇”。
  上网本爆炸性都是奇数的，因为每台上网本都有相同的基本规范，因为如果计算机有超过1.6GHz英特尔原子处理器，1GB的RAM和160GB，Microsoft为标准的非启动Windows许可证的Microsoft为标准的非启动Windows许可证。硬盘。所以这是所有颜色和屏幕尺寸，真的。所有人都要在计算机上运行深层恼人的窗口版本，在计算机上甚至远程声称的计算机上可以替换主PC。在一切结束时，随着筹码不可避免地变得更加强大，足够的笔记本电脑供应商告诉Joana，他们的新上网本的电脑不是她开始称之为“不习俗”的上网本。
 然后iPad在2010年出来，上网本莫名其妙地是一部分计算词汇，即史蒂夫乔布斯通过明确表示上网本糟糕地介绍了iPad。 “问题是上网本并没有更好的事情，”史蒂夫乔布斯在舞台上说，是为了清楚地区分来自上网本的新iPad。这对他很重要！
   甚至没有发生这种情况？这是真的吗？我记得这一切，但我不能判断它是否意味着什么，或者如果我们刚刚相信微软和英特尔是如此神秘的强大，我们必须生活在他们的产品框架和160GB的最大硬盘空间中。有没有人真的买一本网图？我唯一遇到的人谁遇到了上网本是其他科技作家;在一个难忘的贸易秀我的同事Adi Robertson展示了一个巨大的游戏笔记本电脑和一个微小的上网本，两台笔记本电脑都非常适合手头的任务。
 我问乔安娜，谁是WSJ的高级个人技术专栏作家，关于所有这些，谁回答：“让我们在这里清楚。本周苹果的即将到来的活动实际上是关于上网本。十年前，iPad Pro是上网本运动的出现。“她在开玩笑吗？我不知道，她不会告诉我。 
现在，当然，没有任何上网本，但一切都是上网本。 iPad是iPad，具有多种型号，各种尺寸和价格点，以及关于它是否可以替换笔记本电脑的愤怒正在进行的争论。 （见？这是一个上网本。）Chromebook存在，苹果和微软都以熟悉的方式担心它们。 我们被视为类似的设备所包围，将Goofy自定义接口运行到开源操作系统顶部的云服务 - 这是一个回声显示，或谷歌Chromecast，甚至是Oculus任务2.上网本。 英特尔非常需要重新感应，窗户本身减少; 如果有人能够为那种骑行，微软会愉快地称之为“Azure Edge”。
note_en:
There’s  an Apple event next week, and it’s looking fairly likely that we’ll see updated models of the iPad Pro and perhaps the iPad Mini.
 Which is great, because it made me think about the Eee PC, which was either one of the greatest short-lived success stories in tech history or a collective delusion shared by a handful of late 2000s tech bloggers that never actually happened.
   There were two products that arrived in 2007 that fundamentally changed computing: one, of course, was the iPhone. The second, obviously more important product was the $399 Eee PC 701. It originally ran a custom Linux operating system that reviewers loved (  Laptop Mag’s Mark Spoonauer said it was “ten times simpler to use than any Windows notebook”) and was generally heralded as a new kind of computer with tremendous mass appeal. Spoonauer: “Pound for pound, the best value-priced notebook on the planet.”
 Again, this was a weirdo little two-pound plastic laptop that ran  a custom Linux distro that was basically a front for various websites. (We hadn’t invented the phrase “cloud services” yet.)
 Windows getting shown up by Linux was not allowed, so Microsoft did some Microsoft maneuvering, and by January 2008 the Eee PC was running Windows XP instead. It was also part of a larger category called “netbooks,” and we were all made to know what netbooks were.
   A little later, Microsoft created something called Windows 7 Starter, which was a hilarious cut-down version of Windows just for netbooks — you weren’t even  allowed to change the desktop background! — and the netbook explosion was unstoppable. My friend (and Verge co-founder) Joanna Stern built the early part of her career obsessively covering netbooks, first at  Laptop Mag, then  Gizmodo, and then with me at  Engadget.
 Do you remember when the Nokia Booklet 3G was going to reinvent Nokia? You do not, because it didn’t.
 And there was a lot to cover: at one point Joanna noted that Asus had put out  at  least 20 different models of Eee PC in 2008  alone. And that was just Asus! Dell, HP, Lenovo and others all chased after the netbook idea furiously. Do you remember when the  Nokia Booklet 3G was going to reinvent Nokia? You do not, because it didn’t. It was very pretty, though. I asked Joanna about this moment in time, and this is what she sent me:
 “I was basically Bono in this ‘I Still Haven’t Found What I’m Looking For’ video. Eee PC after Eee PC. MSI Wind after MSI Wind. Toshiba whatever it was called after Toshiba whatever it was called. I was constantly looking for a netbook that had a keyboard that didn’t require doll hands, a trackpad that didn’t leave a blister on my thumb, a hard drive that didn’t take three days to open Microsoft Word. It was a constant search for the perfect blend of price, portability and power.”
 Joanna then demanded I embed the actual YouTube video of U2’s “I Still Haven’t Found What I’m Looking For,” which is exactly the sort of “wow that was really important once” energy this blog post needed.
  The netbook explosion was all the more odd because every netbook had the same basic specs, as Microsoft charged more for a standard non-Starter Windows license if a computer had anything more than a 1.6GHz Intel Atom processor, 1GB of RAM, and a 160GB hard drive. So it was all colors and screen sizes, really. All to run a deeply-annoying version of Windows, on a computer that no one was even remotely claiming could replace a primary PC. By the end of it all, as the chips inevitably got more powerful, enough laptop vendors were telling Joanna that their new netbook-like computers  weren’t netbooks that she started calling them “ notbooks.”
 And then the iPad came out in 2010, and netbooks were inexplicably such a part of the computing vocabulary that Steve Jobs introduced the iPad  by explicitly saying that netbooks were bad. “The problem is netbooks aren’t better at anything,” is a real thing Steve Jobs said on stage, in order to clearly distinguish the then-new iPad from netbooks. It was important to him!
   Did any of this even  happen? Is this  real? I remember it all, but I can’t tell if it meant anything, or if we all just believed Microsoft and Intel were so mysteriously powerful that we had to live in their product frameworks and 160GB of maximum hard drive space. Did anyone actually  buy a netbook? The only people I ever met who had netbooks were other tech writers; at one memorable trade show my colleague Adi Robertson showed up with  both a gigantic gaming laptop  and a tiny netbook, two laptops both perfectly ill-suited for the tasks at hand.
 I asked Joanna, who is now a senior personal technology columnist at the  WSJ, about all this, who replied: “Let’s be clear here. Apple’s coming event this week is actually about netbooks. The iPad Pro is an outgrowth of the netbook movement a decade ago.” Was she joking? I don’t know, and she wouldn’t tell me.
 Now, of course, there aren’t any netbooks, but everything is a netbook. The iPad is the iPad, with multiple models at various sizes and price points, and a furious ongoing debate about whether it can replace a laptop. (See? It’s a netbook.) Chromebooks exist, and both Apple and Microsoft worry about them in familiar ways. We are surrounded by appliance-like cheap computers that run goofy custom interfaces to cloud services on top of open-source operating systems — that’s an Echo Show, or a Google Chromecast, or even an Oculus Quest 2. Netbooks. Intel is badly in need of a reinvention and Windows itself is diminished; Microsoft would happily  call its operating system “Azure Edge” if anyone would go along for that kind of ride.
title: 回顾2000年代后期的上网本爆炸，以及iPad，Chromebook和器具样电脑如何采取上网本的位置
title_en: A look back at the netbook explosion of the late 2000s and how iPads, Chromebooks, and appliance-like computers took the netbook's place

Razorpay周一表示，它已经筹集了1.6亿美元的系列融资筹码，以3亿美元的价值为3亿美元，从去年10月1亿美元的估价从“一点点超过10亿美元”。
 新一轮一直由现有投资者新加坡的主权财富基金 -  GIC和Sequoia首都印度联合领导。其他一些现有的投资者包括Ribbit Capital和Tiger Global还参加了新的回合，使Razorpay的迄今为止提高到3.665亿美元。
  Razorpay在线接受，流程和对小型企业和企业支付金钱 - 基本上是美国和其他几个发达市场的一切条纹。但印度初创公司的发言远远超过：近年来，Razorpay推出了一个Neobanking平台来发布公司信用卡（更多在文章的底部），而且还提供业务营运资金。
 随着全球巨型条纹仍然在印度图片中，Razorpay已经成长为成为市场领导者。现在，启动计划计划从亚洲地区的祖国，雷斯巴尔联合创始人和首席执行官中追求其成功，在接受采访时告诉TechCrunch。
 “我们是印度生态系统中最大的支付提供商之一。我们想参加我们在印度到东南亚市场的学习。在财政年度结束之前，我们希望在一个或两个东南亚市场推出，“Mathur说，新的回合使其能够更加自信地探索一些M＆amp的估值。加速增长的机会。
 印度超过500万业务依靠Razorpay的技术来处理付款。这些客户中的一些包括Facebook，电信运营商Airtel，乘车海绵公司OLA，食品交付启动SWIGGY和FINTECH信誉。
 Mathur和Shashank Kumar  - 如图所示 - 在Iit Roorkee College见面。 Duo在那些小企业的早期实现了巨大的困难，在数字化上接受资金，并且现有的付款加工公司没有旨在解决小企业和初创公司的需求。 
解决这个问题成为Razorypay的目标，并且在初期约有11个个人分享了一个单一的公寓，因为联合创始人争先恐后地说服银行家与他们合作。谈话缓慢而且仍然陷入僵局，因为同事们感到无助地对投资者的挑战感到无数，他们在两年前在采访时回忆道。
 故事今天听到razorpay的聆听已经急剧变化。在一个令人惊讶的批评产品的俱乐部房间，数十个开发人员和创始人最近回顾了他们与razorpay的早期互动，以及初创官员如何帮助他们的企业 - 或搬到razorpay的系统伸出时几小时内。
 印度联合创始人Deepak Abbot最近回忆起了一个事件，他的初创公司错过了一个警报，并且加上银行的Snafu，导致初创公司耗尽了资金来支付客户。
 我预计寄生才暂停并思考，但他说让我们加入10万卢比，只是在更安全的一边。虽然我们在聊天@ shashankmehta05时向我发送了一个whatsapp，但金钱已被添加到我们的帐户4/5
 去年，Mathur表示Razorpay的核心业务 - 处理付款 - 快速增长，初创公司将更多地关注建设两个新产品。
 Mathur表示，Mathur表示，Razorpay X现在为15,000名业务提供了大约15,000名业务，比去年10月不到5,000名业务。 Razorpay Capital现在每年为客户提供约8000万美元，从一年前不到4000万美元。贷款罗拉罗巴的持续时间提供三到六个月的范围，票证规模通常为800万至100万印度卢比（10,730美元至13,400美元）。
 Mathur表示，启动将重点关注未来三年的进一步发展这一业务，然后看看初创公司。 “如果只是付款处理业务，我们现在可以公开。但我们的野心是超越 - 成为企业的完整生态系统。在那些新的两侧（Neobanking和Lending），我们很早，“他说。 
初创公司的大面条产品在过去六个月内每月持续增长40-50％。它现在计划在2021年底之前处理超过500亿美元的总付款额。启动也计划聘请一些人。它目前有超过600个公开职位，东南亚市场上有几个。
 星期一的公告是在一片印度初创公司的时候筹集了大量资金，并随着投资者增加了世界第二大互联网市场的有希望的投注的增加。
 印度初创公司社会商业Meesho，Fintech公司克服，电子药学公司药房，千禧一代的成长，商业消息平台Gupshup和社交网络Sharechat本月早些时候达到了独角兽状态。 TechCrunch上周举报了SoftBank正在谈判投资Zeta和Swiggy。
 * Razorpay提供了许多增值服务，例如自动化供应商付款，实时和解和分析，管理订阅，GST发票，设计和创建网站。该启动还开发了一种基于应用的替代金的支付终端（也称为POS）以及用于启用脱机商务的逐个付费。它的产品也是亚马逊新数字套件的一部分，专注于SMB。
note_en:
Razorpay said on Monday it has raised $160 million in its Series E financing round that valued the startup at $3 billion, up from “a little over” $1 billion valuation in the $100 million Series D in October last year.
 The new round has been co-led by existing investors Singapore’s sovereign wealth fund — GIC — and Sequoia Capital India. Some other existing investors including Ribbit Capital and Tiger Global also participated in the new round, which takes Razorpay’s to-date raise to $366.5 million.
  Razorpay accepts, processes and disburses money online for small businesses and enterprises — essentially everything Stripe does in the U.S. and several other developed markets. But the Indian startup’s offering goes much further than that: In recent years, Razorpay has launched a neobanking platform to issue corporate credit cards (more at the bottom of the article), and it also offers businesses working capital.
 With the global giant Stripe still nowhere in the Indian picture, Razorpay has grown to become the market leader. And now, the startup plans to replicate its success from the home country in Southeast Asian markets, Harshil Mathur, co-founder and chief executive of Razorpay, told TechCrunch in an interview.
 “We are one of the largest payments providers in the Indian ecosystem. We want to take the learnings we have in India to the Southeast Asian market. Before the end of the financial year, we want to launch in one or two Southeast Asian markets,” said Mathur, adding that the new round gives it the valuation to more confidently explore some M&A opportunities to accelerate growth.
 More than 5 million businesses in India rely on Razorpay’s technology to process payments. Some of these clients include Facebook, telecom operator Airtel, ride-hailing firm Ola, food-delivery startup Swiggy, and fintech CRED.
 Mathur and Shashank Kumar — pictured above — met at IIT Roorkee college. The duo realized early on that small businesses faced immense difficulties in accepting money digitally and the existing payments processing firms weren’t designed to tackle the needs of small businesses and startups.
 Solving this issue became Razorypay’s goal, and in the early days about 11 individuals shared a single apartment as the co-founders scrambled to convince bankers to work with them. The conversations were slow and remained in a deadlock for so long that the co-founders felt helpless explaining the same challenge to investors numerous times, they recalled in an interview two years ago.
 The stories one hears about Razorpay today have changed dramatically. In a Clubhouse room, known for sharp criticism of products, dozens of developers and startup founders recently recalled their early interactions with Razorpay, and how the startup’s officials helped their businesses start with — or move to — the Razorpay’s system within hours after reaching out.
 Deepak Abbot,  co-founder of Indiagold, recently recalled an incident where his startup had missed an alert, and that coupled with a snafu at the bank, resulted in the startup running out of funds to pay customers.
 I expected Harshil to pause and think but he said let us add 10 lakhs just to be on a safer side. And while we were chatting  @shashankmehta05 sent me a whatsapp that money has been added to our account 4/5
 Last year, Mathur said Razorpay’s core business — processing payments — was fast-growing and the startup would focus more on building the two new offerings.
 Offering an update, Mathur said Razorpay X now serves about 15,000 businesses, up from fewer than 5,000 in October last year. Razorpay Capital is now annually bandying out about $80 million to clients, up from less than $40 million a year ago. The duration of the loan Razorpay provides ranges from three to six months, and the ticket size is typically between 0.8 million to 1 million Indian rupees ($10,730 to $13,400).
 Mathur said the startup will focus on further growing this business in the next three years and then look at taking the startup public. “If it was just the payments processing business, we could go public right now. But our ambitions are beyond — to become the full ecosystem for businesses. And on those new sides (neobanking and lending), we are early,” he said.
 The startup’s marquee offering has grown 40-50% month-on-month in the past six months. It now plans to process over $50 billion in total payment volume by the end of 2021. The startup also plans to hire a number of people. It currently has over 600 open positions, several in Southeast Asian markets.
 Monday’s announcement comes at a time when a slice of Indian startups are raising large amounts of capital at a much frequent pace and increased valuations as investors double down on promising bets in the world’s second largest internet market.
 Indian startups social commerce Meesho, fintech firm  CRED, e-pharmacy firm PharmEasy, millennials-focused  Groww,  business messaging platform Gupshup and  social network ShareChat attained the unicorn status earlier this month. TechCrunch reported last week that SoftBank is in talks to  invest in Zeta and  Swiggy.
 *Razorpay offers a number of value-added services such as automating vendor payments, real-time reconciliation and analytics, managing subscriptions, GST invoicing, designing and creating websites. The startup has also developed an app-based substitute for payments terminals (also known as POS) as well as pay-by-link for enabling offline commerce. Its offering is also part of Amazon’s new Digital Suite focused at SMBs.
title: 基于Bangalore的Razorpay，它帮助SMBS管理数字付款，筹集了GIC和Sequoia Capital India的$ 160M系列E，以3B美元的估价
title_en: Bangalore-based Razorpay, which helps SMBs manage digital payments, raises $160M Series E led by GIC and Sequoia Capital India at a valuation of $3B

纳税报告普通税收最大限度地规定策略难以或不可能在过去一年中蓬勃发展的交易平台上实施
   去年，一个有九家公司的活跃贸易商代顿梁，使用了罗宾尼应用程序的交易。他喜欢他通过推荐的免费股票超过十几个朋友，发现他的手机很容易进行交易。
  然而，最近，他已经停止交易他的机器人账户，其中大约是238,000美元，主要是在特斯拉股票。他说，一个主要原因是税收。
  “罗宾时代将股票的所有股份置于一个大桶中，”43岁的43岁，在伯克利高地，N.J.，并担任物业经理。 “我的2020年资本收益税我困扰着。”
  你是今年经历过税收惊喜的贸易商吗？在下面的评论中告诉我们它。
  由于2020年税收票据到期，新的零售交易员正在唤醒这一事实，即可能困难，往往是不可能的，使税收最大限度地减少抢劫，例如抢劫，瓦哈，韦伯，秉承和公众.com。有些人不允许在IRAS等税收的退休账户内进行交易。交易商还可以难以追踪他们的“洗销售”，如果他们在损失销售同一股票的30天内购买股票，则减少税收优惠。
    莱昂先生这样的投资者的大多数烦恼都是，尽管新的平台的精致技术，但它们并不让人易于部署被称为“特定识别的识别”的税务技术。投资者用它来降低税收，有时会选择卖出的股票以不同的价格买了很多价格，并且没有卖掉它们。 
这就是这个问题的原因。税法允许投资者提供应纳税账户，以利用他们销售股票的损失，以抵消抵消已攀升的股票销售额的收益税收。损失也可以抵消每年高达3,000美元的其他收入，例如工资，例如工资。未使用的损失载有防止未来收入和其他收入。
  “现在我已经完成了税收的研究，我希望我能卖掉我选择的股票，而不是”卖“按钮选择的股票，”波特兰，奥勒的建筑工人和经理Ashton Courson说。 ，在罗宾情绪的账户中大约17,200美元，他没有使用太多，部分原因是税收。
    在传统的经纪公司，销售特定批次的选择。但是Webull，Sofi，Public和Uphold不允许它，罗宾情绪使得困难。这一事实震惊了专业的金钱经理。
  “我认为这是荒谬的。税收是投资回报的巨大成分，它是投资者有一些控制的领域，“明尼阿波利斯的普拉夫策划者的CPA和金钱经理Bill Mulvahill说。
  “这是一个疯狂的 - 这是一个原因是罗宾赫今年是一个福音的一个原因，”日内瓦蓝避风港首都的财务顾问凯文克莱因曼，克里林曼先生表示，他有超过10个不开心的新客户随着罗杰赫，所有人都有征收投诉，其中梁先生。
  智能使用特定批次识别可以最大限度地减少当前的税收票据。说，自2020年9月中旬以来，交易者拥有400美元，650美元和850美元的特斯拉股票。如果此人在最近的价格销售了一些675美元的价格，应征收收益为275美元或每股25美元，取决于销售的股票。这是一个很大的差异 - 如果销售了大约850美元的股票，它甚至更大，损失了每股175美元，可以抵消应税涨幅。
  这些税收结果中的任何一个都可能有意义：投资者在投资组合中其他地方的投资者可能需要采取收益，并且有一个有的人可能想要造成损失。但是许多新的交易平台使这种艰难或不可能做到。 
在罗宾地点的关于税收的网页上，公司并没有告诉客户他们可以选择销售特定批次。相反，它表示销售将在首先第一出，称为FIFO，其中最古老的股票首先出售。如果最古老的股票持续超过一年，FIFO可能会降低税率。在上面的特斯拉示例中，FIFO将使交易者能够想到最糟糕的结果 - 每股275美元的短期收益，以普通收入的税率征税。
    在他们销售股份后送给客户的贸易确认的精细印刷中，罗宾时代确实提供了指定批次的选择。但这个过程很复杂。
  Robinhood的客户无法在销售时指定很多，因为它们通常可以在传统经纪人处。相反，他们在贸易日期后两天后搜索他们的历史和电子邮件客户服务，包括六个数据点，包括日期和价格，包括两天。每个请求都被分配一个案例编号并单独处理。
  一位罗宾尼发言人表示通常试图告诉客户如果他们的要求在七天内取得成功，但税收赛季急于扩大到达30天。由于较高的查询，它还要求客户通过电子邮件了解请求的状态，以确保他们尽快收到信息。
  梁先生说，他发现这个过程如此令人生畏，他没有用它：“挖掘我的订单历史记录太多工作，找到很多，然后希望他们批准销售。”他说，在另一个经纪人的账户中，他说，他的特定批次会因为他的销售股票而弹出，他可以轻松地选择他想要出售的账户。
    Mulvahill先生，他在五家不同传统公司销售了很多销售的经验，这表明，在这些公司通常可以选择许多点击次数。
  罗宾肖的发言人表示，它一直在寻找改善客户体验的方法，但目前没有什么可以分享的关于特定批次识别过程的变化。 
下载您的Wall Street Journal的2021税务指南电子书副本。 （订阅者免费）
  其他新平台根本不允许特定批次ID。 Webull和公共授权FIFO用于销售，而秉承销售最昂贵的股票（称为最高，或HIFO），以及SOFI订单批次，以便输家通常在获奖者之前出售。
  Webull首席执行官Anthony Denier表示，它“希望增加更多的定制”，公众发言人表示，该公司是“研究税收最小化的选择”。 SOFI的发言人表示，纳税细节是“未来发布的路线图”。维护贸易总部Josh Greenwald表示，该公司将考虑在扩大时对税收进行更改。
  税收管理对底线有什么差异？根据最近的一项研究，系统地损失降低纳税收益将纳税申报表提高，平均每年从1926年到2018年为35％税收托架的投资者为每年0.82％。在某些情况下，涨幅可能更大，所以研究的作者是Chapman大学的一个，例如，如果投资者交易小或挥发性股票或短暂的股票。
  丹赫朗，加利福尼亚州圣路易斯奥比斯波的注册会计师，他刚刚为去年为罗宾的客户提供了超过10,000条的客户，说：“我告诉客户在销售时指定批次，或者他们可以征收税收。“
note_en:
Tax Report     Common tax-minimizing strategies are difficult or impossible to implement on the trading platforms that have boomed in popularity in the past year
   Last year Dayton Leong, an active trader with accounts at nine firms, made scores of trades using the Robinhood app. He liked the free stocks he got from referring more than a dozen friends and found it easy to trade on his phone.
  Recently, however, he has stopped trading in his Robinhood account, which has about $238,000 in it, mostly in Tesla stock. He says a major reason is taxes.
  “Robinhood puts all shares of a stock into one big bucket,” says Mr. Leong, age 43, who lives in Berkeley Heights, N.J., and also works as a property manager. “I’m haunted by my 2020 capital-gains tax.”
  Are you a trader who experienced a tax surprise this year? Tell us about it in the comments below.
  With 2020 tax bills coming due, a wave of new retail traders are waking up to the fact that it can be difficult, and often impossible, to make tax-minimizing moves on new brokerage platforms such as Robinhood, Webull, SoFi, Uphold and Public.com. Some don’t allow trading within tax-favored retirement accounts such as IRAs. Traders can also find it hard to track their “wash sales” that reduce tax benefits if they buy a stock within 30 days of selling the same stock at a loss.
    Most vexing for investors like Mr. Leong is that despite the new platforms’ sophisticated technology they don’t make it easy to deploy a tax-wise technique known as “specific-lot identification.” Investors use it to lower their taxes, sometimes significantly, by choosing which shares to sell if they have lots bought at different prices and aren’t selling all of them.
  Here’s why this issue matters. Tax laws allow investors with taxable accounts to use the losses they incur when they sell a stock that’s dropped to offset the taxes on gains from the sales of stocks that have climbed. The losses can also offset up to $3,000 of other income, such as wages, each year. Unused losses carry forward for use against future gains and other income.
  “Now that I’ve done research on taxes, I wish I could sell the shares I choose, not a share selected by the ‘Sell’ button,” says Ashton Courson, age 26, a construction worker and manager from Portland, Ore., with about $17,200 in an account at Robinhood he’s not using much, in part for tax reasons.
    The option of selling specific lots is readily available at traditional brokerage firms. But Webull, SoFi, Public and Uphold don’t allow it, and Robinhood makes it difficult. This fact shocks professional money managers.
  “I think it’s absurd. Taxes are a huge component of investment returns, and it’s an area where investors have some control,” says Bill Mulvahill, a CPA and money manager at Trailhead Planners in Minneapolis.
  “It’s crazy—and it’s one reason Robinhood has been a boon to my business this year,” adds Kevin Kleinman, a financial adviser with Blue Haven Capital in Geneva, Ill. Mr. Kleinman said he has more than 10 new clients who were unhappy with Robinhood, and all had tax complaints, among them Mr. Leong.
  Smart use of specific-lot identification can minimize current tax bills. Say that a trader holds Tesla shares bought at $400, $650 and $850 apiece since mid-September, 2020. If this person decided to sell some of the winners at a recent price of $675, the taxable gain would be either $275 or $25 per share, depending on which shares were sold. That’s a big difference—and it’s even bigger if some $850 shares were sold, bringing a loss of $175 per share that could offset taxable gains.
  Any of these tax results could make sense: An investor with losses elsewhere in a portfolio might want to take gains, and one with gains might want to take losses. But many of the new trading platforms make this hard or impossible to do.
  On Robinhood’s  webpage about tax lots, the firm doesn’t tell customers they have the option of selling specific lots. Instead, it says sales will be on a first-in-first-out basis, known as FIFO, in which oldest shares are sold first. While FIFO could lower tax rates if the oldest shares have been held longer than a year, it might not. In the Tesla example above, FIFO would give the trader conceivably the worst outcome—a short-term gain of $275 per share, taxed at  the rates for ordinary income like wages.
    In the fine print of trade confirmations sent to customers after they’ve sold shares, Robinhood does offer the option of specifying lots. But the process is complicated.
  Customers at Robinhood can’t specify a lot at the time of sale, as they typically can at traditional brokers. Instead, they search their history and email customer service with six datapoints, including dates and prices, before the trade settles two days after the trade date. Each request is assigned a case number and handled individually.
  A Robinhood spokeswoman said it typically tries to tell customers if their request was successful within seven days, but the tax-season rush has temporarily expanded the wait to up to 30 days. Because of a high volume of inquiries, it’s also asking customers to email to find out the request’s status to ensure they’ll receive the information as quickly as possible.
  Mr. Leong says he finds the process so daunting he hasn’t used it: “It’s too much work to dig through my order history to find lots and then hope they approve the sale.” In another account at a traditional broker, he says, his specific-lot history pops up as he’s selling shares, and he can easily select which ones he wants to sell.
    Mr. Mulvahill, who has experience selling lots online at five different traditional firms, says that at those firms typically lots can be chosen and sold with a few clicks.
  A spokeswoman for Robinhood says it’s always looking for ways to improve its customer experience but has nothing to share at this time about changes to the process of specific-lot identification.
  Download your copy of The Wall Street Journal’s 2021 tax-guide ebook. (Free for subscribers)
  Other new platforms don’t allow specific-lot ID at all. Both Webull and Public mandate FIFO for sales, while Uphold sells the most expensive shares available (called highest-in-first-out, or HIFO), and SoFi orders lots so that losers are typically sold before winners.
  Anthony Denier, chief executive of Webull, says it’s “looking to add more customization,” and a spokeswoman for Public says the company is “studying tax-minimizing options.” A spokesman for SoFi says tax-lot detail is “on the road map for future releases.” Josh Greenwald, head of trading for Uphold, says the company will consider making changes to tax offerings as it expands.
  What difference does tax management make to the bottom line? According to a  recent study, systematically taking losses that reduced taxable gains boosted after-tax returns by an average of 0.82% a year from 1926 to 2018 for an investor in a 35% tax bracket. The gains could be larger under some circumstances, says one of the study’s authors, Terence Burnham of Chapman University, such as if an investor traded small or volatile stocks or shorted them.
  Dan Herron, a CPA in San Luis Obispo, Calif., who just prepared a return for a client who made more than 10,000 trades on Robinhood last year, says: “I tell clients who trade to specify lots when they sell, or they could get hosed on taxes.”
title: 像罗宾情绪，瓦哈特，SOFI等经纪服务如何使税收最大限度地减少诸如难以或不可能实施的税收
title_en: How popular brokerage services like Robinhood, Webull, SoFi, and Uphold can make tax-minimizing strategies difficult or impossible to implement

随着基于10nm Ice Lake处理器的英特尔的第三代Xeon可扩展平台推出，英特尔已升级了该公司的一部分，这是大笔资金。在过去的几年里，英特尔已经从一个以CPU为中心的公司枢转到一个以数据为中心的公司，利用了其越来越多的产品线，以便在数据中心心态构建。随着新服务器平台在过去一周的推出，英特尔正在为10NM，PCIe 4.0和加密加速构建的企业堆栈进行控制。
  除了新的10nm冰湖Xeons，英特尔还拥有Optane持久存储器，200千兆以太网，Stratix和Agilex FPGA，具有3D NAND的高性能SSD，基于Optane的存储，AI硬件与Movidius和Habana，Tofino网络交换，Easic技术，即将推出的x e图形。据Lisa Spelman，英特尔的CVP和Xeon和Memory Group的GM表示，这组组合使公司成为竞争无法启用的解决方案的独特地位。
  在这次采访中，我们询问英特尔的产品，新的加速特征的范围，对英特尔客户来说真正重要的是，英特尔如何接近它的路线图，因为从冰湖到蓝宝石急流的快速跟随。
    Lisa Spelman分类是我所说的“英特尔救生员”，在公司近20年来。她的角色已经进入了英特尔数据中心业务和Xeon产品组合的一个面孔，讨论了产品线的技术方面，也详细讨论了业务和营销策略。她以前的角色包括在CIO，客户服务总监以及数据中心营销总监以及数据中心营销总监提供技术顾问。
      IC：Xeon冰湖终于拥有阳光明媚的海湾核心设计。服务器行业有优化，用于AI，最多40个核心，新的安全功能和更高的内存性能。历史上英特尔已经将其Xeon可扩展的处理器带到市场的每个角落 - 这仍然是真实的新冰湖Xeon平台？
  LS：我认为你已经达到了一些主要的功能和福利。我们在这里做的是我们正在更新平台，我们正在更新处理器，我们立即更新我们的一系列投资组合，我们认为提供了巨大的客户价值。对您在市场的每个角落中有英特尔的问题 - 我们认为这是我们的差异化因素。我们真的专注于击中边缘工作负载并通过网络传递，并将进一步的网络转换驾驶到Xeon [从边缘到云]。我们正在在数据中心的核心中提供，我们正在云和高性能计算提供。我们继续寻求扩大我们为客户提供的服务和能力，并且只需在其使用案例中提供平台一致性。
  [注意]我从不打电话给Xeon一个服务器产品！我努力工作，让它被改为被称为真正的数据中心产品，现在它实际上甚至延伸到那之外，坐在这么多的边缘和粗犷的环境中。所以我在每个基站和每个电话杆上看到一个Xeon来骄傲和喜悦！ 
IC：这些英特尔扮演的额外市场，他们是收入增长的大机会吗？这是先前的首席执行官鲍勃天鹅以及最近新的首席执行官Pat Gelsinger在一起的重要特征。
  LS：这是一个收入机会，这是客户的机会。它允许我们解决更多的需求，实际上它允许我们在客户的成功中发挥更重要的作用。我们不仅仅是在数据中心，我们正在跨越我们的客户正在寻求推动自己的价值的所有方式，他们自己的货币化，因此它实际上是一些有趣的东西。
      IC：在过去几代内部的重点是对服务器客户的常见日常工作负载的核心性能。英特尔目前似乎专注于解决方案交付，通过可扩展的生态系统的其他部分，如Optane DCPMM 200系列，新的800系列以太网，Optane SSD和3D NAND，Agilex FPGA，Easic，以及Dlboost和Oneapi的软件堆栈。您是否可以进入客户在生态系统中的需求如何从简单地购买到解决方案优化的购买超越核心的解决方案？
  LS：我仍然将Xeon描述为所有通用解决方案的最通用目的！它可以真的可以运行任何东西，它会给你一个盒子里的一切都有一个良好的东西。我们花费了巨大的努力和资源，我们如何在特定领域改进，我们瞄准那些更高的[市场]的增长领域。你提到了人工智能，这是我们在硬件方面投资的一个区域，这是超级重要的，但该软件至少相等（如果不是更重要的）来调整性能。 [启用]整个投资组合来提供解决方案的心态可能是我们最大的变化之一，[尤其是]如何与我们的客户互动，并通过查看我们的[客户]并与他们一起使用更多的整体要求。因此，我们的需求聚集肯定改进并变得更加全面，然后我们已经建立了一些解决方案能力和解决方案产品。
  我们讨论了市场就绪解决方案，特别是针对边缘，我们讨论了云和企业和网络和HPC使用情况的选择解决方案。我们实际上具有类似类型的程序，这些程序较少专注于我们顶级云服务提供商客户的品牌，创建云工程工作，这些工作旨在将这些自定义CPU与Optane性能配对，或以太网800系列性能，真实帮助他们推出更多的基础架构购买。
  我们已经利用我们投资组合的FPGA部分的一个区域也在进入智能NIC，这也是一个不断增长的兴趣领域，并为我们提供了真正全面地解决客户的机会，以及货币化。他们想在核心之上做。
      LS：我仍然认为观众的核心和想要听到这个的行业，并希望看到它看起来像什么。我不希望我们远离利用这些基准。当您使用JU Xeon产品或全部Xeon平台时，我还试图将团队[朝向]与实际真实的结果配对基准。我们希望能够[语音]这种翻译，因为客户在解决方案样式视图周围的旅程中处于不同的景点。我认为英特尔继续推动绩效，我们将继续报告并讨论标准化的基准，但我们将增加投资组合可以做些什么的更多信息。所以我认为满足我们的客户和观众是很重要的，而不是刚刚决定我们的位置。 
IC：英特尔的服务器产品自Xeon Phi日期以来一直在使用AVX-512，然后在Skylake上介绍了Xeon处理器。我们现在也看到AVX-512首次在消费者桌面处理器上滚出。对于普遍存在的AVX-512，仍然存在一些困惑，特别是在涉及到企业和云服务提供商（CSP）代码基础时。您与该功能看到哪些趋势，以及英特尔在冰湖等平台前进的程度如何是有关的。
  LS：AVX-512是如此出色的功能。它对使用它的客户具有巨大价值，有时我读取您阅读观众关于喧闹辩论的评论，他们将对AVX-512的价值有关。但我告诉你，没有笑话，在推出之前一半和半左右，我正在研究我们的一个地理位置的交易清单。 70％的交易胜利，我们的Salesforce为此赢得的原因是AVX-512。优化是真实的。
  虽然发生了什么，如到目前为止的AVX-512和未来SGX，当您启动并宣布新的东西时，尽管我们的10,000多个软件工程师和我们的生态系统的报道，但它很难得到所有的堆栈利用其中一个硬件功能。完全启用它真的很难。它已经习惯了[AVX-512]，还有更多的工作要做。
  所以每个可以从AVX-512中受益的客户都可以访问，因为他们的软件堆栈完全准备好了？不，但我们的巨额数量[更多客户]今年，无法在2011年的AVX-512，2012年或2015年或2017年.Enablement刚刚在一年继续增长。
  如果我们将其链接到软件保护扩展（SGX），我们现在拥有Xeon可扩展的较大尺寸的固定。我们在我们的Xeon-E产品系列市场上有了SGX，少几代，这允许巨大的行业准备和采用。它还允许您看到谁真的很感兴趣，并在建立机密计算基金会方面非常感兴趣。所以现在我们是[将功能移动]进入较大的插座，占地面积更大，飞地大小，人们可以开始实际扩展他们的部署，因为它们已经准备好了。
  因此，您可以查看已经有机密计算实例的云服务提供商，如微软，IBM，阿里巴巴，百度，然后您看看这些企业，如加拿大皇家银行，PayPal，Visa，UCSF，德国医疗保健 - 这些公司都很好正在进行部署，因为他们有时间，现在他们可以将其转化为更大的规模。
  这一切都会回到我如何考虑如何在整体路线图管理中与我们的团队合作的方式。我们已经看到了，我们正在驾驶，过去几年的转变更具体地关注有意义的特色进步，[而不是简单地]一种洗衣书的功能列表。我们更集中并规定了看着我们的路线图。如果我们添加一个功能，我们必须考虑使用该功能的内容，以及如何使客户受益，而不是简单地将其放在硬件中。这是一个在英特尔思想和英特尔思考的转变，但我认为它不仅会受益于自己，而是我们的客户也会受益。他们会看到更多的东西[随着时间的推移]，它在到达时市场将更准备好。 
IC：SGX，软件保护扩展，是您在Xeon-E中的某种程度上有一段时间。既然该功能正在向上移动到更宽的Xeon堆栈中，英特尔将分段与飞地大小进行分段。一些SKU有8 GB的入围，有些人做64 GB，有些人在做512 GB。您是否可以说出这种分割对英特尔事项的原因，也可以谈论平台是否有启用的其他安全元素？
  LS：在飞地大小分割上，这是关于给客户的选择和机会。他们有机会通过什么类型的部署来思考，他们将需要的部署，这种类型的飞地大小[他们需要]，以及他们试图管理的类型的数据类型。 [它是关于]他们尝试更强大的安全应用类型的应用程序，并且这种分段给出了它们可选的。
  [值得注意的是，这是我们第一次将该选择带入市场，所以我们将学习。我们将获得客户的反馈，以及我们在我们的云和OEM合作伙伴中合作的人，因为我们构建了SKU堆栈的样子。但现在[与新功能]我们将获得所有现实世界的客户部署信息，然后我们将在前进时进行调整。您已经通过了足够的推出，并看到我们如何启动新功能，我们从事努力，然后我们随着时间的推移而改进它并解决市场采用。因此，我们认为这将为客户提供一个很棒的基础，我们将看到更多客户从Xeon E到Xeon可扩展部署的现有Poc（概念证明）转换。我们还将看到一系列新的概念证明，为客户使用两个套接字系统等待真正的市场准备。所以我认为它会使我们有机会在市场中学习和发展[与我们的客户]。因此，这是我们最大的安全进步之一 - 在客户的软件安全组合中添加了基于硬件的基于硬件。
  我们还增加了进一步的加密加速，这是英特尔已在几年或更长时间内投入的一个区域。我们认为这是我们真正的差异化因素。我们有一个辉煌的团队，专注于这个空间，并在硬件和加密算法上工作，也适用于硬件的软件支持。它也是与客户真正紧张的领域。正如您在[Ice Lake Xeon-SP]发布的情况下，比赛正在播放尽可能多的数据加密，并将其保持在更大的安全性。我认为这将继续在非常重要的功能集中建立，并使客户受益于我们已经受益的更大能力。
      IC：历史上讲英特尔与Xeon平台发射出来的时候谈到客户，它从一个插座到八个插座一直缩放。我们现在有一个带有第三代Xeon可扩展的情况，我们有一个分裂，在这里，您已经在10个nm上有两个套接，在14 nm的四到八个插座上有冰湖。你能解释拆分市场的原因吗？是否存在技术限制，或者是客户需求，或者混合中还有其他东西吗？这表明我们在未来可能看到的是什么，或者英特尔将再次聚结？
  LS：我认为我们在投资组合中建立了更大的灵活性。几个星期前，你会看到我们创造了许多我们创造的一些东西首席执行官Pat Gelsinger在英特尔释放的内容。 Pat谈论[能够]满足客户需求，这可能不太具体。 [与我们的客户进行讨论，]我们有机会刷新并带来一些新的功能，即在冰湖前面的四个套接字和超越空间。我们选择利用该机会，添加一些新功能（如Bfloat16），并满足我们在将冰湖进入市场的空间中的一些非常具体的大容量客户要求。
  鉴于传统的四个套接字企业市场的规模，以及特定客户，将我们的投资加倍，我们在冰湖或第3根Xeon可扩展到启用另外四个插座平台，这么快似乎可以为平台提供的价值进行管理。因此，我们已将正确的能力与第三次第3章（Cooper Lake）的第一部分进行刷新，以满足几个非常重要的客户要求。 [这使我们能够首先将生态系统专注于其冰湖两个套接字系统，而是继续前进到我们的下一代蓝宝石急流，这将覆盖整个堆栈。 
所以[Cooper Lake]实现了一个受客户需求推动的机会。将[双插座和四套接和四套接市场]分开是一种永恒的策略，但随着我们提高我们的能力和步伐，将新技术带到市场，我们仍然可以看出传统的四个插座市场的频率级别的速度。
    IC：谈到那些选择客户，在过去在数据中心创新日，我记得一个幻灯片，有一个饼图，指示英特尔的所有Skylake Xeons。其中一半以上的图表定制了特定的客户需求，尤其是这些大型高调客户，云服务提供商和超高音器。你会说你还是50％+定制那些大客户的定制吗？你能谈谈如何发展？
  LS：它仍然超过50％，我会说你会继续看到那种成长。我们也在尝试做的是为所有这些市场添加一定程度的自定义。
  横跨SKU的主要路线图，您将看到SGX Chandave尺寸的机会，但我们也有SKU，云优化了VM利用率，我们有网络和NFV优化的SKU，媒体处理优化的机会，以及一些长寿和更加粗鲁的使用寿命边缘工作负载的案例处理器。因此，我们甚至在主要的SKU堆栈中提供了一种定制程度，因为那些对其工作量的基础架构的那种精确定位的那种精确定位。
  我们仍然提供50％+定制零件，并将继续发展我们的定制卷。随着时间的推移，我们在这个空间中增强了我们的能力，我有一个单独的团队，我们投入了我们的主要客户的自定义SKU。这允许他们真正调整和目标的环境。它始于几个旋钮来调整，现在它已经增长，我们有能力和能力和能力和愿意在定制到IP水平。
  我们使用这些SKUS，当我们制造它们时，我们创建它们，并且在我们部署它们时，我们实际上将其留给我们的客户，了解他们想要暴露[新闻或客户]的详细信息。共同优化通常在他们如何对竞争对手区分如何对抗竞争对手的重要性。因此，我们认为他们有很多能力确定他们想要与世界分享多少。对于英特尔，我们专注于主要的性能基准，我们所做的一切都在每个人都提供的主线。
  IC：所以你带来了新的单独的SKU是有趣的。除了以前的网络聚焦的那些，您现在拥有媒体专注的SKU。那是什么？这是什么？什么是特别的？ 
LS：如果您考虑工作负载，我们正在尝试瞄准正确的核心计数，右频率，然后是单核与全核turbo之间的混合。它也是关于那里的高速缓存，以及热量对这些客户的样子。
  在这种情况下，在媒体处理和优化的工作负载中，我们有一个专注于此工作负载和这些关键客户以及收集要求的团队。要求来自CSP运营商，在线游戏运营商以及所有这些类型的客户。 [我们希望启用]最理想的配置，用于保证服务质量，并允许在可能进入卸载加速器之前交付尽可能多的性能。因此，对于那些是我们有那些旋钮的硬件配置，我们可能会打开这个sku。
  我们要做的第二件事是我们将我们的软件为他们融为一体。因此，当我们进行编码支持，软件支持时，我们尝试确保它们更新以利用该特定的SKU和该配置。您显然会在您选择的任何SKU上获得福利，但我们将对此处理器上的代码进行更高的调谐和优化。因此，在标准基准测试中，您可能会获得X性能，但如果您将媒体SKU版本与之相反，则可能会获得X加上性能提升，可能是10％，具体取决于确切的情况。
  IC：产品堆栈中的这个液体冷却处理器是什么！是关于HPC的吗？
  LS：你永远不知道谁在那里的实验！但是的，绝对集中了一个向HPC。我仍然没有个人有机会在坦克的东西中蘸上我的手，拉开Xeon，但我很期待！
  ic：对不起，当我说液体冷却时，我以为这意味着某种闭环液体毡

note_en:
With the launch of Intel’s Third Generation Xeon Scalable platform based on 10nm Ice Lake processors, Intel has upgraded a part of the company that makes the BIG money. For the last few years Intel has been pivoting from a CPU-centric company to a Data-centric company, leveraging the fact that more and more of its product lines are built towards the datacenter mindset. With the launch of the new server platform in this past week, Intel is gearing itself up for an enterprise stack built on 10nm, PCIe 4.0, and cryptographic acceleration.
  Alongside the new 10nm Ice Lake Xeons, Intel also has Optane persistent memory, 200 gigabit Ethernet, Stratix and Agilex FPGAs, high-performance SSDs with 3D NAND, Optane-based storage, AI hardware with Movidius and Habana, Tofino network switching, eASIC technology, and upcoming X e graphics. This portfolio combined, according to Lisa Spelman, Intel’s CVP and GM of the Xeon and Memory Group, puts the company in a unique position of offering solutions that the competition can’t enable.
  In this interview, we ask about Intel’s offerings, the scope of new accelerative features, what really matters to Intel’s customers, and how Intel is approaching its roadmap given the fast follow on from Ice Lake to Sapphire Rapids.
    Lisa Spelman is categorically what I call an ‘Intel Lifer’, having spent almost 20 years in the company. Her role has evolved very much into one of the faces of Intel’s Data Center business and the Xeon product portfolio, discussing technical aspects of the product lines but also business and marketing strategy in detail. Her previous roles have included being an internal analyst, technical advisor to the CIO, the Director of Client Services, and Director of Datacenter Marketing.
      IC: Ice Lake Xeon is finally here with its Sunny Cove core design. There are optimizations for the server industry, for AI, up to 40 cores, new security features, and higher memory performance. Historically Intel has taken its Xeon Scalable processors to every corner of the market - is this still true with the new Ice Lake Xeon platform?
  LS: I think you’ve hit on some of those key features and benefits. What we’re doing here is we’re updating the platform, we’re updating the processor, and we’re updating a bunch of our portfolio all at once, which we think delivers a tremendous amount of customer value. To your question about having Intel in every corner of the market - we think it is a differentiator of ours. Where we are really focused starts with hitting edge workloads and delivering through the network, and driving further network transformation onto Xeon [from edge to cloud]. We’re delivering in the core of the datacenter, and we’re delivering in cloud and high performance computing. We’re continuing to seek to expand the services and capabilities that we can offer for customers, and just deliver platform consistency across so many of their use cases.
  [Note that] I never call Xeon a server product! I worked so hard to get it changed into being called a true datacenter product and now it’s actually even extended out beyond that, to sitting in so many edge and ruggedized environments. So I take great pride and joy in seeing a Xeon on every base station and on every telephone pole!
  IC: These extra markets that Intel plays in, are they a big opportunity for revenue growth? It’s been a big feature of what previous CEO Bob Swan and what new CEO Pat Gelsinger has been saying recently.
  LS: It’s a revenue opportunity and it’s a customer opportunity. It allows us to address more of their needs, and actually it allows us to play a more important role in our customer success. We’re not just in the datacenter, we’re spanning all of the ways in which our customers are seeking to drive their own value, their own monetization, and so that’s actually some of the funnest stuff to be part of.
      IC: A lot of focus over the past generations has specifically been on the core performance on common everyday workloads for server customers. Intel is now seemingly focused on solution delivery, enabled through the other parts of the Scalable ecosystem such as Optane DCPMM 200 series, new 800-series Ethernet, Optane SSD and 3D NAND, Agilex FPGAs, eASICs, and the software stack behind DLBoost and OneAPI. Can you go into how customer demand in the ecosystem is shifting from simply a core purchase to a solution-optimized purchase that goes beyond the core?
  LS: I still describe the Xeon as the most general purpose of all the general purpose solutions! It can literally run anything, and it’ll give you a good out of the box experience on everything. We have spent a tremendous amount of effort and resources in how we can improve in specific areas, and we do target those higher [market] growth areas. You mentioned artificial intelligence, which is an area where we’re investing both on the hardware side, which is super important, but the software is at least equal (if not more important) to tune for performance. [Enabling] that entire portfolio to deliver a solutions mindset has probably been one of our biggest changes, [especially] how to engage with our customers, and by looking at our [customers] and working with them on much more holistic requirements. So our requirements gathering has definitely improved and become much more comprehensive, and then we’ve built some solutions capabilities and solutions offerings on top of it.
  We have talked about Market Ready Solutions, especially targeted to the Edge, and we talked about Select Solutions for cloud and enterprise and network and HPC use cases. We actually have similar types of programs that are less focused on the branding for our top cloud service provider customers as well, creating cloud engineering efforts that are focused on pairing those custom CPUs with the Optane performance, or the Ethernet 800 Series performance, and really helping them drive more value out of their infrastructure purchases.
  An area where we’ve been utilizing the FPGA portion of our portfolio is in the move into smart NICs as well, which is a growing area of interest and gives us an opportunity to really holistically address our customer infrastructure management, as well as the monetization they want to do on top of the core.
      LS: I still think there is a core of the audience and the industry that wants to hear that, and wants to see what it looks like. I don’t want us to walk away from utilization of those benchmarks. I’m also trying to drive the team [towards] the market that is pairing the benchmark with the actual real-world result when you use either just a CPU Xeon product or the entirety of the Xeon platform. We want to be able to [voice] that translation because customers are in different spots in their journey around a solution-style view. I think it’s important for Intel to continue to drive performance up, and we will continue to report on and discuss standardized benchmarks, but we’re adding into it a lot more of a holistic view of what the portfolio can do. So I think it’s important to meet our customers and our audiences where they’re at, instead of just dictating where we’re at.
      IC: Intel’s server products have been using AVX-512 since the days of Xeon Phi, and then it was introduced on Xeon processors from Skylake. We are now also seeing AVX-512 roll out on the consumer desktop processors for the first time. There’s still some confusion as to how pervasive AVX-512 is, especially when it comes to Enterprise and Cloud Service Provider (CSP) code bases. What pick-up trends are you seeing with the feature, and how critical it will be as Intel progresses forward with platforms like Ice Lake.
  LS: AVX-512 is such a great feature. It has tremendous value for our customers that use it, and sometimes I marvel when I read your audience’s comments about the raucous debate they will have about the value of AVX-512. But I tell you, and no joke, that a week and a half or so before launch I was looking at a list of the deal wins in one of our geographies. 70% of those deal wins, the reason listed by our salesforce for that win was AVX-512. Optimization is real.
  What happens though, as with AVX-512 so far and with SGX in the future, is that when you launch and announce something new, despite our 10,000+ software engineers and our coverage of the ecosystem, it can be hard to get all of the stack to take advantage of one of those hardware features. It’s really hard to get that completely enabled. It has taken us years [for AVX-512] and there is more work to be done.
  So has every customer that could benefit from AVX-512 had access to that because their software stack is completely ready? No. But we have a tremendous amount [of more customers] this year that weren’t able to utilize AVX-512 in 2011, or 2012, or 2015, or 2017. Enablement just keeps growing, year on year.
  If we link it to Software Guard Extensions (SGX), we now have the larger size enclave available on Xeon Scalable. We’ve had SGX in the market on our Xeon-E product line for a few generations, and that has allowed a tremendous amount of industry preparation and adoption. It also allows you to see who is really deeply interested and committed in building that confidential computing foundation. So now we are [moving the feature] into the larger socket, with a larger footprint, a larger enclave size, and people can start to actually scale their deployments pretty quickly because they’re ready.
  So you look at cloud service providers that already have confidential computing instances, like Microsoft, IBM, Alibaba, Baidu, and then you look at these enterprises, like Royal Bank of Canada, PayPal, VISA, UCSF, German Healthcare - these companies are well underway to deployment because they have had the time and now they can move it into even greater scale.
  It all leads back to how I think of how I’m working with our team on overall roadmap management. We have seen, and we are driving, a shift over these past few years to focus more concretely on meaningful feature advancement, [rather than simply] a laundry list of features. We are much more focused and prescriptive about looking at our roadmaps. If we add a feature, we have to consider what it would take to have that feature utilized, and how would it benefit a customer, rather than simply what it takes to put it in the hardware. That’s a shift in an Intel mindset and Intel thinking, but I think it will benefit not only ourselves, but our customers as well. They will see more stuff [over time], and it will be more ready in the market when it arrives.
      IC: SGX, Software Guard Extensions, is something you have had in Xeon-E for a while. Now that the feature is moving up into the wider Xeon stack, Intel has segmentation with enclave size. Some SKUs have 8 GB maximum enclaves, some doing 64 GB, and some doing 512 GB. Can you speak to reasons why this segmentation matters to Intel, but also can you speak about other security elements that the platform is enabling?
  LS: On the enclave size segmentation, this is about giving customers choice and opportunity. They have the opportunity to think through what type of deployment that they are going to need, what that type of enclave size [they will need], and what type of data they are trying to manage. [It is about] what type of applications are they trying to more robustly secure, and this segmentation gives them that optionally.
  [It is worth noting that] it is the first time we are bringing that choice into the market, and so we’ll learn. We will get feedback from our customers, and those with which we have worked with in our ecosystem of cloud and OEM partners as we build out what the SKU stack looks like. But now [with the new feature] we will get all of that real-world customer deployment information, and then we will make adjustments as we go forward. You’ve been through enough of these launches with us, and seeing how we launch new features, where we start with an effort and then we refine it over time as we see and address the market adoption. So we think this will give customers a great foundation to start with, and we will see even more customers transition their existing PoCs (proof of concepts) from Xeon E to Xeon scalable deployments. We will also see a new wave of proof of concepts for customers that were waiting for a little bit more of that true market readiness with a two socket system. So I think that it will give us an opportunity to learn and grow in the market together [with our customers]. So that is one of our biggest security advancements - adding that hardware based addition to our customer’s software security portfolio.
  We are also adding further crypto acceleration, and this is an area that Intel’s been invested in for several years to a decade or more.  We do see it as a true differentiator for us. We have a brilliant team that focuses in this space, and works on both the hardware and the cryptographic algorithms as well software support for the hardware. It’s also an area of really tight collaboration with our customers. As you heard me say at [Ice Lake Xeon-SP] launch, the race is on to get as much data as possible encrypted, and keep it under greater security. I think that this is going to continue to build upon very important feature sets, and enable greater capability that our customers benefit from already.
      IC: Speaking about the customers, historically when Intel comes out with a Xeon platform launch, it scales all the way from one socket to eight sockets. We’ve now got a situation where with 3rd Generation Xeon Scalable, we have a split, where you’ve got Ice Lake for up to two sockets on 10 nm, and Cooper Lake on four to eight sockets on 14 nm. Can you explain reasons for splitting the markets? Are there technical limitations, or is it customer demand, or is there something else in the mix? Is this indicative of what we might see in the future, or will Intel be coalescing back again?
  LS: I think that we are building an even greater flexibility in our portfolio. You will see a lot of optionality that we create with some of the stuff CEO Pat Gelsinger talked about at Intel Unleashed a few weeks ago. Pat talked about [being able to] meet customer needs that might not be quite so specific. [In discussions with our customers,] we had an opportunity to refresh and bring forth some new capabilities in that four socket and above space ahead of Ice Lake. We made the choice to take advantage of that opportunity, add some new capabilities (like BFLOAT16), and meet some very specific high volume customer requirements that we had in that space ahead of getting Ice Lake out into the market.
  Given the size of the traditional four socket enterprise market, and the specific customers, doubling our investment in Ice Lake or 3rd Gen Xeon Scalable to enable another four socket platform so quickly seemed like too much to manage for the value that platform would offer. So we had delivered the right capabilities with the first portion of the 3rd Gen portfolio (Cooper Lake) for a refresh to meet a couple of really big customer requirements. [This enables us] to have the ecosystem focus on their Ice Lake two socket systems first, but then moving on to our next generation of Sapphire Rapids which will cover the whole of the stack.
  So [Cooper Lake] realized an opportunity we had, driven by customer demand. It was not an eternal strategy to separate the [dual socket and quad socket markets], but as we increase our ability and pace to bring new technology to market, we may still look at how often the traditional four socket market needs that level of refresh.
    IC: Speaking about those select customers, in the past at the Data Centric Innovation Day, I remember a slide which had a pie chart indicating all the Skylake Xeons that Intel makes. More than half of them in that graph were customized to a specific customer needs, especially these big high profile customers, the cloud service providers and the hyperscalers. Would you say that you’re still 50%+ customizing for those sorts of big customers? Can you talk about how that has evolved?
  LS: It’s still more than 50%, and I would say that you will continue to see that grow. What we’re also trying to do is add a level of customization for all those markets.
  Across the main roadmap of SKUs you will see the SGX enclave size opportunity, but we also have SKUs that are Cloud optimized for VM utilization, we have Networking and NFV  optimized SKUs, the media processing optimized opportunities, and some long life and more ruggedized use case processors for edge workloads. So we’re trying to provide a level of customization even within the main SKU stack for those that have that kind of precision targeting of their infrastructure for their workload.
  We do still deliver 50%+ customized parts, and it will continue to grow our customized volume. Over time we have grown our capabilities in this space, and I have a separate team for this that we’ve invested in that manages our custom SKUs with our major customers. This allows them to really tune and target for their environments. It started out as a couple of knobs to tune, and now it has grown and we have the capability and ability and willingness to do full on customization up to the IP level.
  What we do with these SKUs, when we manufacture them, we create them, and as we deploy them, we actually leave it up to our customers for how much of the detail that they want to expose [to the press or customers]. That co-optimization is often a lot of their own significance in how they differentiate themselves against competitors. So we consider that a lot of their ability to determine how much they want to share with the world. For Intel, we focus on the main performance benchmarks, and everything we do is on the main line available to everyone.
  IC: So it’s interesting that you bring up the new separate SKUs. Beyond the network-focused ones, which you had in the previous generation, you now have a media focused SKU. What’s that all about? What’s that got in it? What is special?
  LS: If you think about the workload, we’re trying to target the right core count, the right frequency, and then the mix between single-core versus all-core turbos. It’s also about how much cache is in there, and what the thermals look like for those customers.
  In this case, across the media processing and optimized workloads, we have a team that is focused on this workload and these key customers and as they gather requirements. Requirements come from the CSP operators, online gaming operators, and all of those types of customers. [We want to enable] the most ideal configuration for guaranteeing quality of service and allowing for as much performance to be delivered before moving potentially to offload accelerators. So for those are the hardware configurations we have those knobs we might turn to create that SKU.
  The second thing we’ll do is we focus our software work for them. So as we do encoding support, software support, we try to make sure they’re updated to take advantage of that specific SKU and that configuration. You will obviously get benefits across any SKU you choose, but we’ll do the greater tuning and optimization for code targeted on this processor. So on a standard benchmark you might get X performance, but if you take the media SKU version against that, you will likely get X plus a performance boost, could be 10%, depending on the exact case.
  IC: What’s this liquid cooled processor in the product stack all about! Is that for HPC?
  LS: You never know who is out there that’s going to experiment as well! But yes, definitely focused that one towards HPC. I still haven’t personally had the opportunity to dip my hand in a tank of something and pull out a Xeon, but I’m looking forward to it!
  IC: Sorry when I say liquid cooled, I thought it meant some sort of like a closed loop liquid coole
......

已成为Apple设备盒和一些Apple产品的一个短语，由Apple在加利福尼亚州组装在中国。“这八个词可能对普通的苹果消费者来说并不重要，但他们足以总结一下现在的公司价值超过2万亿美元成为消费者小工具最大的卖家之一。现在正在进行对Apple供应链和产品制造设备的逐步改变。这一变化的一个副产品是越来越多的苹果产品盒，不再包含在中国组装的加利福尼亚州的苹果设计的苹果产品盒。“这一变化对苹果在2020年代将如何接近产品制造的影响。
  大多数分析师和专家都降落在苹果的定制硅努力，作为公司成功的界限 - 解释苹果公司如何从同龄人中脱颖而出的最重要因素。虽然Apple的硅Prowess无疑是公司的关键差异化因素，但十年长的赌注不是公司在今天所在的基本原因。相反，一个人必须介绍一个过程和文化，使苹果的硅努力成为可能。
 最终，Apple的设计主导文化是公司扩展其安装基地的最负责任的因素，以至于超过十亿人。苹果公司与客户组成的关系不仅仅是任何联系，而是公司世界中最强大，最忠诚的联系。 Apple对设计（我们如何使用产品）并被证明是正确的。相信这项技术的力量太大，没有被获得的感知和自然智能使苹果的兴趣成为一个异常值。其他公司现在正在努力模仿Apple的设计主导思维和文化，以不同程度的成功。
  虽然那些居住在美国的那些人可能不会在加利福尼亚州设计的“加利福尼亚州”组件中，但是苹果的根源和遗产与硅谷交织在一起。 Apple可以被描述为中心为中心的公司。尽管宣布奥斯汀的主要扩张计划，以及在包括西雅图（蒲团亚马逊雇员），圣地亚哥（蒲式青队员工），Culver City（Poach Hollywood Talents），匹兹堡（自治系统），纽约（媒体和出版），波士顿（机器人），波特兰（与硬件相关的努力）和博尔德（可能与健康有关的努力），一切都回到加利福尼亚州库伯蒂诺的Apple Park。 Apple Park仍然是阳光，所有这些卫星办公室和校园都在围绕它旋转。
 特别是从苹果上回来的一步，在几年前结束的硅谷或胸部的时代。然而，这并不意味着硅谷被移除为世界上最集中的创新来源之一和世界上的新时代。最近的一些尝试将一些绘制美国的其他部分。如迈阿密和奥斯汀，因为新的硅谷可以制作伟大的推文线程和博客帖子，但对于每个人都“逃离”硅谷，有多个人准备成为一个替代品。
  “在中国加利福尼亚州的苹果设计的苹果”的争议部分是“在中国组装”。对于一些产品而言，Apple依靠“中国制造”。近年来，苹果对中国供应链和制造设备的广泛关系已被标记为主要责任。头条新闻和叙述普遍倾向于苹果对中国的方法，房屋约占Apple用户的15％至20％。许多专家都希望Apple简单地关闭了中国的商店并放弃了该国的用户。
 随着世界两次经济动力之间的关系恶化，苹果已被描述为卡在中间。当然，这种简单的描述缺乏细微差别。当谈到中国时，苹果的立场并不像西方媒体想要人们思考一样危险。 Apple能够在中国的高级品牌地位，品牌在中国的科技场景中仍有严重影响力。这不是普通话，说在中国销售的大多数非iPhone智能手机都是由iPhone的“启发”。通过Apple Watch和Airpods可以说是可带上的可穿戴物品的可带性能。 
除了强大的品牌之外，苹果在中国的力量站立时，苹果有一些关键的事情。最终是中国最大的私营雇主背后的司机意味着什么。苹果产品不仅在中国制造的中国发售，而且该国负责制造送给其他国家的苹果产品。作为Apple供应链和制造设备的家，使中国能力和站在其与美国越来越多的国家的经济战斗中，包括印度。
  2020年12月，我在苹果揭开了较低成本的扬声器后不久的情况下订购了两个主页迷你扬声器。在打开盒子之前，我对扬声器的初步观察之一。首页迷你盒的背面没有含有在加利福尼亚州的苹果设计的通常“苹果公司”短语“。相反，以下内容打印在框中：
  谣言已将新的首页迷你迷住于越南组装。即便如此，在盒子上看到越南是一个醒目者。从Homepod Mini回来，我们看到Apple踏上了远离中国的更广泛的产品制造。目前，以下产品或将在中国以外组装。
  （虽然Mac Pro已被组装在美国。多年来，该设备以这样的低位销售，令人难以困扰苹果已拥抱美国制造业。）
 少数东南亚国家现在处于制造苹果产品的立场。其中一些是由于各国政府越来越多地适应外国投资。另一个因素是Apple的长期和重要的业务合作伙伴Foxconn，表现出许多在中国以外的自身的业务和足迹多元化的努力。
 在这一制造业转型的核心，一个简单的原则是引导苹果：挡住电力。通过在中国以外的产品组装多样化，苹果最终会达到各国政府和装配者彼此。 Apple成为中国，印度和东南亚国家之间增加的经济竞争中最大的受益者之一。 Apple继续利用印度更友好和容纳环境，为该国带来一个不断增长的iPhone生产。
 至于Apple移动的一些更精细的策略细节，而不是宣布像“我们正在向中国移动所有iPhone生产一样的大变化”，这是西方媒体多年来，苹果正在采取更实用和聪明的方法。公司仍然小心不要破坏现有的装配设备。绝大多数产品组装仍然存在于中国。 Apple在中国以外地区寻找能够为较新产品的装配处理，这些产品相对于iPhone的低得多批量出售。这些决定涉及不仅仅是产品装配商（Foxconn，Pegatron，Luxshare，Wistron）的全面检查，而且还涉及主要供应商与多元化努力合作的能力。 Apple受益于拥有靠近汇编者的资源和资产。 
俯视道路，预计期望苹果产品的增长百分比将是合理的，将在中国以外组装。苹果甚至可能会把制造业带回美国，我们会谈论比Mac Pro大得多。而不是在阿拉巴马州，格鲁吉亚或田纳西州制造的iPhone和iPad，而不是苹果汽车将在第三方拥有的庞大植物中产生一天，而是包含苹果公司拥有的机械和设备。
  苹果悄然而逐步向我们展示我们在加州在中国组装的苹果设计的阶段“将进化。该公司仍然在中国投资，这可能不会在近期改变。然而，通过将产品组装逐渐多样化为其他国家，苹果最终显示了世界其供应链含有比想象的评论家更多的可选性。世界各地的苹果业务经济权力之间的战斗将成为2020年代的关键主题。
 通过独家日常更新（每天3个故事，每天12个故事，在整个周内接收我的分析和透视。可用于阿瓦隆成员。要注册和更多信息，请访问会员资格页面。
note_en:
One phrase that has become a fixture on Apple device boxes and some Apple products is “Designed by Apple in California Assembled in China.” Those eight words may not mean much to the average Apple consumer, but they sufficiently sum up how a company now worth more than two trillion dollars became one of the largest sellers of consumer gadgets. A gradual change to Apple’s supply chain and product manufacturing apparatus is now underway. One byproduct of this change is an increasing number of Apple product boxes that no longer contain “Designed by Apple in California Assembled in China.” The change has implications for how Apple will approach product manufacturing in the 2020s.
  Most analysts and pundits have landed on Apple’s custom silicon efforts as the defining source of the company’s success – the single-most important factor in explaining how Apple has been able to stand out from its peers. While Apple’s silicon prowess is undoubtedly a key differentiator for the company, the decade-long bet isn’t the fundamental reason why the company is where it is today. Instead, one has to look at the processes and culture that made Apple’s silicon efforts possible in the first place.
 Ultimately, Apple’s design-led culture is the single-most responsible factor behind the company’s ability to expand its installed base to more than a billion people. The relationships that Apple has formed with its customers aren’t just any connections but rather some of the strongest and most loyal connections in the corporate world. Apple placed  a big bet on design (how we use products) and was proven right. Believing that technology is too powerful of a force to enjoy without acquired perception and natural intelligence made Apple an outlier. Other companies are now trying to emulate Apple’s design-led thinking and culture with varying degrees of success.
  While those residing in the U.S. may not make much out of the “in California” component of “Designed by Apple in California,” Apple’s roots and heritage are intertwined with Silicon Valley. Apple can be described as a headquarters-centered company.  Despite announcing major expansion plans in Austin, and new presences / expansion in in a handful of U.S. cities including Seattle (poach Amazon employees), San Diego (poach Qualcomm employees), Culver City (poach Hollywood talent), Pittsburgh (autonomous systems), New York (media and publishing), Boston (robotics), Portland (hardware-related efforts), and Boulder (possibly related to health-related efforts), everything comes back to Apple Park in Cupertino, California. Apple Park remains the sun with all of these satellite offices and campuses revolving around it.
 Taking a step back from Apple in particular, the era when it was either Silicon Valley or bust ended years ago. However, that doesn’t mean Silicon Valley has been displaced as one of the most concentrated sources of innovation and new age thinking in the world. Recent attempts by some to paint other parts of the U.S. such as Miami and Austin as new Silicon Valleys may make for great tweet threads and blog posts, but for every person “fleeing” Silicon Valley, there are multiple people ready to be a replacement.
  The controversial part of “Designed by Apple in California Assembled in China” is “Assembled in China.” For some products, Apple has relied on “Made in China.” In recent years, Apple’s extensive ties to China regarding its supply chain and manufacturing apparatus have been labeled as major liabilities. Headlines and narratives are universally slanted against Apple’s approach to China,  home to approximately 15% to 20% of Apple’s users. Many pundits want Apple to simply close up shop in China and abandon its users in the country.
 As relations between the world’s two economic powerhouses deteriorate, Apple has been described as being stuck in the middle. Of course, such a simplistic description lacks nuance. When it comes to China, Apple’s position is not nearly as perilous as western media wants people to think. Apple has been able to maintain its premium brand status in China and the brand remains heavily influential in China’s tech scene. It’s not outlandish to say that most non-iPhone smartphones sold in China are heavily “inspired” by the iPhone. The same can be said about wearables being “inspired” by Apple Watch and AirPods.
 In addition to a strong brand, Apple has a few key things going for it when it comes to its power standing and positioning in China. Being ultimately the driver behind the largest private employer in China means something. Not only are Apple products destined for sale in China made in China, but the country has been responsible for manufacturing Apple products sent to other countries. Being home to Apple’s supply chain and manufacturing apparatus gives China power and standing in its economic battle with the U.S. and increasingly other countries, including India.
  In December 2020, I ordered two HomePod mini speakers shortly after Apple unveiled the lower-cost speaker. One of my initial observations about the speakers materialized before opening the boxes. The back of the HomePod mini box did not contain the usual “Designed by Apple in California Assembled in China” phrase. Instead, the following was printed on the box:
  Rumors had pegged the new HomePod mini as being assembled in Vietnam. Even then, seeing Vietnam on the box was an eye-opener. Taking a step back from the HomePod mini, we see Apple embark on a broader move away from China for product manufacturing. The following products are currently, or will be, assembled outside of China.
  (While the Mac Pro has been assembled in the U.S. for years, the device sells in such low numbers that it’s tough to say Apple has embraced U.S. manufacturing.)
 A handful of countries in Southeast Asia are now in a position to manufacture Apple products. Some of this is due to governments increasingly accommodating foreign investment. Another factor is Apple’s long-time and vital business partner, Foxconn, showing a renewed effort to diversify its own business and footprint outside of China.
 At the heart of this manufacturing transformation, one simple principle is guiding Apple: retaining power. By diversifying product assembly outside of China, Apple ends up pitting both governments and assemblers against each other. Apple stands to be one of the largest beneficiaries from increased economic rivalry between China, India, and Southeast Asia countries.  Apple continues to take advantage of India’s more friendly and accommodating environment to bring a growing portion of iPhone production to the country.
 As for some of the finer strategy details found with Apple’s move, instead of announcing a big change like “we are moving all iPhone production out of China,” which western media has been demanding for years, Apple is taking the more practical and intelligent approach. The company remains careful not to disrupt its existing assembly apparatus. The vast majority of product assembly remains in China. Apple has looked outside of China to handle assembly for newer products that sell in much lower volumes relative to the iPhone. Such decisions involve a comprehensive examination of not just product assemblers (Foxconn, Pegatron, Luxshare, Wistron) but also the ability of key suppliers to work with the diversification efforts. Apple benefits from having resources and assets close to assemblers.
 Looking down the road, it is reasonable to expect a growing percentage of Apple products will be assembled outside of China. It is even likely that Apple will bring manufacturing back to the U.S. and we would be talking something much larger than the Mac Pro. Instead of iPhones and iPads being made in Alabama, Georgia, or Tennessee, it is more likely that  Apple Cars will one day be produced in sprawling plants that are owned by third parties but contain Apple-owned machinery and equipment.
  Apple is quietly and gradually showing us that the phase “Designed by Apple in California Assembled in China” will evolve. The company remains heavily invested in China, and that likely won’t change in the near term. However, by gradually diversifying product assembly into other countries, Apple ends up showing the world that its supply chain contains much more optionality than critics imagined. The battle between the world’s top economic powers for Apple’s business will be a key theme to watch in the 2020s.
 Receive my analysis and perspective on Apple throughout the week via exclusive daily updates (3 stories per day, 12 stories per week). Available to Above Avalon members. To sign up and for more information on membership, visit the   membership page .

西雅图 - 失去竞标的联盟代表亚马尔，阿拉伯贝塞姆尔的亚马逊仓库工作人员，现在，本月早些时候正在寻求推翻选举结果，以法律提出，电子商务巨头不当地迫使工人反对工会。
  零售，批发和百货商店联盟将与国家劳工关系委员会周五提出反对，这是一个触发听证程序来解决其索赔的举措。劳动法要求原子能机构在投票的15个工作日内或在4月30日之前设定该听证会。如果联盟成功，劳工委员会可以致电新的选举。
 联盟表示，当它失去高调投票4月9日时，它会提出异议，这是一个不平衡的失败，其中1,798名员工反对工会和738个支持它。那个胜利的边际留下了另外505个选票，因为他们不会改变结果。
   Rwdsu所谓的选举中的亚马逊的策略“构成了雇员阻止自由和不开心行使的行为，”增加行动“构成一个地方，以便在一边设定选举。”
  亚马逊发言人希瑟·克诺克斯质疑rwdsu在贝塞尔员工的后果中的挑战绝大多数反对工会。
 “而不是接受这些员工的选择，该联盟似乎决定继续歪曲事实，以便推动自己的议程，”诺克斯在一封电子邮件发表中说。 “我们期待法律程序的下一步。”
     虽然许多联盟的指控呼应声称在组织驾驶过程中索赔，但它也制作了一系列新的指控。在其申请中，Union指定了亚马逊向工会支持者解雇了“用于在非工作领域传递联盟授权卡”。 RWDSU还指责公司在强制会议期间挑战亚马逊的争论挑战亚马逊的论据。联盟没有命名为工人或披露具体的学科。 
RWDSU包括之前在亚马逊上升级的费用，在仓库前面突然出现在投票开始后的仓库。联盟通过信息法案的自由获得了电子邮件，表明亚马逊员工按下邮政服务安装邮箱，就像七周的邮件投票开始。
  在其申请中，工会争论邮箱，邮箱没有美国邮政服务标记，创造了亚马逊在收集和计数选票中发挥作用的印象，可能会影响他们的投票。并指出，NLRB，该列出选举规则，拒绝了亚马逊的要求将投票箱放在仓库中的人入住的投票。劳工委员会对冠状病毒大流行期间亚马逊工人和机构工作人员的安全引起了担忧。
  亚马逊以前曾为邮箱放置辩护，说它为工人做出了投票，并且只有邮政服务就可以访问它。
  联盟还声称，亚马逊成功地让当地县官员更改了仓库外的交通灯的时间不正确地抑制其在其班次结束时互动的能力。亚马逊表示要求改变改善交通流量。
  联盟还据称，亚马逊的财务报价使诱惑不快乐的工人戒掉的措施，对杂草的激励不当。亚马逊已经反驳了这些指控，并指出它开始在2014年辞职的财务激励措施，并扩展了所有北美仓库工作人员的倡议。
 RWDSU申请还声称亚马逊受到威胁的裁员，甚至关闭仓库，如果工人投票赞成联盟代表。它涉及亚马逊“贬低”它在仓库中的大流行相关的社会疏远政策，允许反工会员工竞选，但防止接合工人有同样的访问。
